meta.name: LSNS-6 Lubben Social Network Scale
meta.description: The LSNS-6 is a 6-item self-report scale designed to assess social isolation in older adults by measuring the size and closeness of their family and friendship networks. A total score below 12 is considered to indicate social isolation risk.
meta.author: Lubben et al. (2006)
meta.reference: Lubben J, Blozik E, Gillmann G, Iliffe S, von Renteln Kruse W, Beck JC, Stuck AE. Performance of an abbreviated version of the Lubben Social Network Scale among three European community-dwelling older adult populations. Gerontologist. 2006;46(4):503-513.
meta.instruction: The following questions are about your social network. Please answer each question as accurately as possible.

questions.text: How many relatives do you see or hear from at least once a month?
How many relatives do you feel at ease with that you can talk about private matters?
How many relatives do you feel close to such that you could call on them for help?
How many friends do you see or hear from at least once a month?
How many friends do you feel at ease with that you can talk about private matters?
How many friends do you feel close to such that you could call on them for help?

ranges.label: At Risk for Social Isolation
ranges.description: Your total score suggests you may be at risk for social isolation. Limited social networks can impact both mental and physical health. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Consider reaching out to family or friends more regularly
Look for community activities or groups to join
Talk to a healthcare provider about resources for social engagement
Volunteering can be a great way to build social connections
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Moderate Social Network
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of social engagement. There may be room to strengthen your social connections. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Continue nurturing your existing relationships
Consider expanding your social network
Maintain regular contact with family and friends
Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern

ranges.label: Strong Social Network
ranges.description: You have a strong social network with good connections to both family and friends. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your strong social connections
Your social network is a valuable resource for well-being
Consider supporting others who may be socially isolated
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

family.label: At Risk
family.description: Your family network is limited. You may benefit from strengthening family connections. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

family.label: Moderate
family.description: You have a moderate family network. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

family.label: Strong
family.description: You have a strong and supportive family network. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

friends.label: At Risk
friends.description: Your friend network is limited. You may benefit from building more friendships. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

friends.label: Moderate
friends.description: You have a moderate friend network. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

friends.label: Strong
friends.description: You have a strong and supportive friend network. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life
